user_query:
I’m trying to refactor a tiny greeting utility toward more inclusive defaults and to prepare a clear handoff for our team. Please work in this workspace with the provided inputs and produce the requested outputs.

Inputs to use:
- input/app/greet.py (current implementation)
- input/app/__init__.py (package marker)
- input/tests/test_greet.py (unit tests that currently fail)
- input/analysis/static_analysis.csv (static analysis findings to prioritize)

What I need:
1) Run the tests, capture and analyze the failures, then refactor for inclusivity:
   - Run: python -m unittest discover -s input/tests -t input
   - Save the full raw test output before any changes to output/reports/tests_before.txt and briefly annotate the key failure(s) at the top of that file.
   - Refactor input/app/greet.py to:
     • Default to a neutral, inclusive greeting that avoids gendered terms (e.g., do not use "sir", "ma'am", or "guys").
     • Ignore honorifics when choosing language.
     • Accept an optional pronoun parameter in both the function and CLI (e.g., --pronoun) that can be used to personalize the greeting if provided, but keep backward compatibility with the existing arguments.
   - Re-run the same test command. Save the full raw test output after your changes to output/reports/tests_after.txt and ensure all tests pass.
   - Also save the refactored module as a copy to output/refactored/greet.py (the updated contents should match the refactored input/app/greet.py).

2) Filter, sort, and rank the inclusivity-related findings:
   - From input/analysis/static_analysis.csv, filter rows where code starts with "INC".
   - Group by code and compute: count (number of rows per code) and top_severity for that code using the precedence High > Medium > Low.
   - For each code, include a representative example_message (e.g., the first message you encountered for that code).
   - Write a ranked summary to output/analysis/inc_issue_summary.csv with columns: code,count,top_severity,example_message.
   - Sort the rows primarily by top_severity (High first, then Medium, then Low), then by count descending, then by code ascending.

3) Prepare meeting notes with action items:
   - Create output/meetings/refactor_planning_notes.md that includes:
     • A brief summary of the refactoring changes and why inclusivity matters for user-facing text.
     • A "Prioritized Issues" section listing the top 5 items from output/analysis/inc_issue_summary.csv in the ranked order.
     • An "Action Items" section with at least 3 concrete items (each with a suggested owner placeholder and due date placeholder) informed by the ranked issues and test results.

4) Draft a team update email:
   - Write output/communications/refactor_update_email.md that:
     • Explains what changed, why we’re emphasizing inclusive language, and how to try the updated CLI (include an example command).
     • Uses an empathetic tone inviting feedback, especially from folks adapting to change, and highlights how to suggest improvements.
     • Includes 3–5 bullet points summarizing key outcomes (e.g., tests now pass, default greeting is neutral, pronoun flag added).

5) Brief notes on diagnostics:
   - In output/reports/notes.md, include:
     • The exact test command you ran and any error messages observed initially (quote the relevant lines).
     • A 2–4 sentence explanation of how you interpreted the failures and the specific changes you made in input/app/greet.py.
     • A short note explaining how you computed the ranking from input/analysis/static_analysis.csv and any tie-break rules used.

Acceptance:
- Tests pass per output/reports/tests_after.txt.
- output/analysis/inc_issue_summary.csv correctly reflects only INC* codes with the specified ranking and columns.
- Meeting notes and email are present at the specified paths and align with the refactoring and ranked issues.
- The refactored module is saved both in-place (input/app/greet.py) and at output/refactored/greet.py.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
